key_insight: A hybrid machine-learning and deep-learning pipeline was built to predict lethal outcomes after acute myocardial infarction and identify key biomarkers linked to complications.
clinical_relevance: If validated, this kind of model could help clinicians flag high-risk MI patients earlier and support faster, more consistent decision-making.
method: Preprocessed data with missing-value handling and imbalance correction using SVMSMOTE, ADASYN, and class-weighted methods.
Selected important features with wrapper and embedded methods, then standardized inputs.
Combined Logistic Regression, Random Forest, LightGBM, Bagging SVM, and an artificial neural network for final prediction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation across precision, recall, and other performance metrics to compare model options for clinical use.
watch_item: Performance may not generalize without external validation, and imbalanced clinical data can still bias predictions despite resampling.
paper_title: Predicting Lethal Outcome (Cause) And Understanding Key Biomarkers Linked With Acute Myocardial Infarction Using Deep Artificial Neural Network And Ensemble Of Machine Learning Methodologies
